MICKEY HART APPEARED BACKSTAGE DURING THE BREAK, HOLDING A COWBELL. BILL KREUTZMANN OBJECTED. HART JOINED ANYWAY — HIS FIRST TIME ON STAGE WITH THE GRATEFUL DEAD SINCE FEBRUARY 1971, NEARLY FOUR YEARS. THE TWO-DRUMMER CONFIGURATION THE BAND WOULD RIDE FOR THE NEXT TWENTY YEARS BEGAN IN THIS ROOM ON THIS NIGHT. COLD RAIN AND SNOW OPENED — THE FIRST TIME IN A YEAR. GOOD LOVIN' APPEARED IN THE THIRD SET, UNPLAYED SINCE PIGPEN LAST SANG IT IN MAY 1972 — "NOBODY WAS SURE WHAT TO DO." STELLA BLUE WAS "BRIGHT, BEAUTIFUL." A CRYPTICAL ENVELOPMENT RIFF SURFACED IN WHARF RAT THAT HADN'T BEEN HEARD SINCE 1971 — OLD GHOSTS APPEARING ON THE LAST NIGHT. BILL GRAHAM PULLED THE ENTIRE CREW ONSTAGE FOR THE FINAL ENCORE. THE BAND PLAYED AND WE BID YOU GOODNIGHT. GRAHAM THREW A PARTY THAT LASTED UNTIL DAWN. THE HIATUS LASTED NEARLY TWO YEARS. THE WALL OF SOUND WAS DISMANTLED AND NEVER REASSEMBLED. WHEN THE DEAD RETURNED IN 1976, THEY USED A DIFFERENT SYSTEM ONE-FOURTH THE SIZE. RELEASED ON STEAL YOUR FACE AND THE GRATEFUL DEAD MOVIE SOUNDTRACK.

FEBRUARY 26, 1977 — SWING AUDITORIUM, SAN BERNARDINO. A TEN-THOUSAND-SEAT BARN ON THE NATIONAL ORANGE SHOW FAIRGROUNDS — NO AIR CONDITIONING, FLAT FLOOR, AND TERRIBLE SOUND. THE ROLLING STONES HAD MADE THEIR AMERICAN CONCERT DEBUT HERE IN 1964. FOUR YEARS AFTER THIS SHOW, A TWIN-ENGINE CESSNA WOULD NOSEDIVE THROUGH THE ROOF, KILLING TWO PEOPLE AND ENDING THIRTY-TWO YEARS OF ROCK AND ROLL. THIS WAS THE FIRST SHOW OF 1977. TERRAPIN STATION AND ESTIMATED PROPHET BOTH DEBUTED ON THE SAME NIGHT — TWO SONGS THE BAND HAD NEVER PLAYED LIVE BEFORE. ROBERT HUNTER WROTE TO GARCIA AFTERWARD: "THE FIRST NIGHT IT WAS PLAYED, TOOK ME ABOUT AS CLOSE AS I EVER EXPECT TO GET TO FEELING CERTAIN WE WERE DOING WHAT WE WERE PUT HERE TO DO." ALSO THE FIRST LIVE HELP ON THE WAY > SLIPKNOT! > FRANKLIN'S TOWER AS A COMPLETE SUITE. THE BAND HADN'T PLAYED IN OVER TWO MONTHS BUT CAME OUT SWINGING. AN EYEWITNESS NOTED "MORE HELL'S ANGELS THAN I'D SEEN AT ANY OTHER DEAD SHOW — BECAUSE IT WAS SAN BERNARDINO." STEVE PARISH WAS RIGGING JERRY INTO A MOOG SYNTHESIZER THROUGH KEITH'S SETUP — THE FIRST USE OF THE MU-TRON ENVELOPE FILTER THAT WOULD DEFINE GARCIA'S SOUND FOR THE REST OF THE DECADE. GENERAL ADMISSION: SEVEN DOLLARS AND FIFTY CENTS. RELEASED AS DAVE'S PICKS VOLUME 29.

MARCH 18, 1977 — WINTERLAND ARENA, SAN FRANCISCO. NIGHT ONE OF THREE AT THE HOME BASE. THE FIRST-EVER FIRE ON THE MOUNTAIN — AND THE FIRST TIME SCARLET BEGONIAS AND FIRE WERE PLAYED BACK TO BACK, A PAIRING THAT WOULD BE PERFORMED NEARLY TWO HUNDRED AND FIFTY MORE TIMES OVER THE NEXT EIGHTEEN YEARS. NEITHER SONG KNEW LIFE WITHOUT THE OTHER FROM THIS NIGHT FORWARD. PETE TOWNSHEND OF THE WHO WAS REPORTEDLY IN THE AUDIENCE WITH HIS "JAW ON THE FLOOR" — "OOOOH, I GOTTA GET THIS SOUND." THE SUGAREE IS WIDELY CONSIDERED ONE OF THE BEST THE DEAD EVER PLAYED — ONE FAN WHO'D SPENT FOURTEEN YEARS LOOKING FOR A CD-QUALITY VERSION DESCRIBED THE "MONSTROUS BUILD-UP AND IMMEDIATE COLLAPSE AT THE END OF THE SECOND SOLO" AS "EXHAUSTING." THE BAND ALSO ATTEMPTED THE ONLY LIVE PERFORMANCE OF "AT A SIDING" FROM THE TERRAPIN STATION SUITE — THEY STRUGGLED WITH THE COMPLICATED DRUM AND GUITAR SWITCHES AND GAVE UP AFTER ABOUT THIRTY SECONDS. AN EYEWITNESS WHO'D ARRIVED FROM NEW YORK ON A GREYHOUND BUS WITH HIS BICYCLE IN A BOX SAID PEOPLE WERE "JUST GAPING AT EACH OTHER" AFTER THE SHOW. HIS NEXT SHOW WOULD BE CORNELL.

MARCH 19, 1977 — WINTERLAND ARENA. NIGHT TWO. "BY FAR MY FAVORITE BIG RIVER TO DATE — KEITH GOES IN AT ABOUT TWO MINUTES." THE TERRAPIN > PLAYING > SAMSON > PLAYING FIRST SET CLOSER IS "ABSOLUTELY FACE MELTING." ONE REVIEWER: "ONCE I HEARD THIS SHOW I FORGOT ABOUT MARCH 18TH AND ITS FIRST EVER SCARLET > FIRE ALMOST ENTIRELY." "THE EAST COAST TOUR GETS ALL THE ATTENTION, BUT DON'T MISS OUT ON THE SMOKIN' HOT WINTERLAND SHOWS FROM THIS YEAR."

APRIL 22, 1977 — THE SPECTRUM, SOUTH PHILADELPHIA. THE ROOF HAD BLOWN OFF TWICE IN ITS FIRST FIVE MONTHS — ONCE DURING AN ICE CAPADES PERFORMANCE, THEN AGAIN TWO WEEKS AFTER THE REPAIR. JOHNNY CARSON JOKED ABOUT IT ON THE TONIGHT SHOW. THE BUILDING WAS TECHNICALLY BANKRUPT UNTIL 1971. BY 1977 IT HAD EARNED THE NICKNAME "THE HOUSE THAT ROCK BUILT" — THE AIR THICK WITH POT SMOKE, THE FLOOR STICKY WITH BEER SOLD THROUGHOUT THE SHOW, THE CONCOURSE LINED WITH PEOPLE WHO'D PASSED OUT BEFORE THE FIRST NOTE. THE DEAD WOULD PLAY HERE FIFTY-THREE TIMES — MORE THAN ANY OTHER MUSICAL ACT, INCLUDING SPRINGSTEEN. THIS WAS THE OPENING NIGHT OF THE LEGENDARY SPRING 1977 NORTHEAST RUN — TWO AND A HALF WEEKS THAT PRODUCED SOME OF THE MOST CELEBRATED TAPES IN THE BAND'S HISTORY. AN EYEWITNESS DESCRIBED HIS FIRST LISTEN TO TERRAPIN: "WHAT THE FUCK IS THIS?! WITH GARCIA ROCKING OUT, ACTUALLY MOVING AROUND AND GETTING DOWN, DOWN LIKE ALMOST TO HIS KNEES." THERE WAS NO ENCORE.

APRIL 23, 1977 — SPRINGFIELD CIVIC CENTER. EYEWITNESS: "FAVORITE OF THE SEVENTY SHOWS I SAW BETWEEN 1973 AND 1995. GENERAL ADMISSION, ON A RAINY COOL DAY. EVERY SPRING AROUND THIS DATE I PLAY MY CASSETTE COPY IN MY CAR." SECOND NIGHT OF THE LEGENDARY NORTHEAST TOUR. "STARTS AT AMAZING, THEN TAKES IT UP A NOTCH — NAUGHTY AND NICE, TOUGH AND SENSITIVE, PRIMAL AND SOPHISTICATED." THE SLIPKNOT HAS KEITH "DOING REALLY NEAT STUFF ON THE SYNTH." THE FRANKLIN'S TOWER "JUST BLOWS UP, TAKING THE CROWD ALONG FOR A RIDE INTO A WHIRLPOOL OF CRAZED JOY." FIRST-TIMER (LLUCY): "MY FIRST SHOW. RODE THE RAIL IN FRONT OF JERRY."

APRIL 25, 1977 — CAPITOL THEATRE, PASSAIC, NEW JERSEY. THE BUILDING HAD OPENED IN 1926 AS A VAUDEVILLE HOUSE AND DEGRADED INTO A PORN THEATER BY THE EARLY SEVENTIES. JOHN SCHER, A YOUNG PROMOTER FROM WEST ORANGE, SAW HIS CHANCE WHEN THE FILLMORE EAST CLOSED IN 1971. HE NEGOTIATED WITH THE PORN DISTRIBUTOR — A HUNDRED DOLLARS A NIGHT ABOVE STANDARD REVENUE — AND FOR A WHILE THE CAPITOL LIVED A DUAL LIFE: KUNG FU MOVIES BY DAY, THE GRATEFUL DEAD BY NIGHT. HE HIRED THE FILLMORE EAST'S OLD CREW AND GAVE A SHABBY ROOM WORLD-CLASS PRODUCTION VALUES. THIRTY-ONE HUNDRED SEATS ON A SINGLE FLOOR — BIGGER THAN A CLUB, SMALLER THAN AN ARENA, AND WITH ACOUSTICS THAT ONE REGULAR CALLED "SUPER." SCHER RAN TWO SHOWS A NIGHT ON WEEKENDS, PUTTING SIX THOUSAND PEOPLE THROUGH IN A SETTING THAT FELT INTIMATE. NIGHT ONE OF TWO. THE WHARF RAT IN THE SECOND SET IS CONSISTENTLY CALLED ONE OF THE BEST OF THE YEAR.

APRIL 27, 1977 — CAPITOL THEATRE, PASSAIC. NIGHT TWO. THE FORMER PORN THEATER. EYEWITNESS (TOUR DIARIST): "THE PLACE IS VIBRATING IN ANTICIPATION. THE ST. STEPHEN DEBUT LEADS THE WAY THROUGH A FEROCIOUS BACK END OF THE SET." THE HALF-STEP: "JERRY CRUSHES IT — FORGET ALL THE OTHER SPRING '77 VERSIONS, THIS IS MY NEW NUMBER ONE." EYEWITNESS: "I WAS A HIGH SCHOOL JUNIOR. A FEW DAYS LATER I SAW THE CORNELL SHOW AND WAS MORE ALERT, HAVING JUST A FEW TOKES. THAT WAS THE SHOW THAT MADE ME A DEADHEAD." FM BROADCAST CIRCULATES.

APRIL 29, 1977 — THE PALLADIUM, 14TH STREET AND IRVING PLACE, NEW YORK CITY. FORMERLY THE ACADEMY OF MUSIC — A 1926 MOVIE PALACE DESIGNED BY THOMAS LAMB, NOW A FADED, GRIMY ROCK VENUE WITH KUNG FU MATINEES AND THREE-THOUSAND-FOUR-HUNDRED SEATS. CBGB WAS FIFTEEN BLOCKS SOUTH. THE RAMONES, BLONDIE, TALKING HEADS, AND PATTI SMITH WERE ALL PLAYING WITHIN A FEW SUBWAY STOPS. SON OF SAM WAS KILLING PEOPLE. THE CITY WAS NEARLY BANKRUPT. TWO MONTHS FROM NOW, THE BLACKOUT WOULD TRIGGER CITYWIDE LOOTING AND HELP BIRTH HIP-HOP IN THE BRONX. THE DEAD BROUGHT SAN FRANCISCO PSYCHEDELIC OPTIMISM INTO A CITY THAT FELT LIKE IT WAS COMING APART. NIGHT ONE OF THREE AT THE PALLADIUM. THE HELP > SLIPKNOT! > FRANKLIN'S TOWER RUNS NEARLY TWENTY-NINE MINUTES — ONE EYEWITNESS CALLED IT "A PSYCHEDELIC HURRICANE." THE CROWD SANG ALONG WITH JERRY ON FRANKLIN'S TOWER. SOMEONE RANG A BELL AT THE 4:18 MARK AND YOU CAN HEAR IT ON THE TAPE.

APRIL 30, 1977 — THE PALLADIUM. NIGHT TWO. EYEWITNESS (TOUR DIARIST): "AFTER LAST NIGHT'S SHOW I'M TOTALLY STOKED. THE ST. STEPHEN DEBUT HAS ME DANCING LIKE THERE'S NO TOMORROW." SAINT STEPHEN — ONLY THE FOURTH SINCE ITS RETURN — LED THE WAY THROUGH "A FEROCIOUS BACK END OF THE SET WITH A RIGHTEOUSLY NASTY NFA AND A SWEET STELLA BLUE." THE HALF-STEP ENDING JAM: "ABSOLUTELY FEROCIOUS — JERRY CRUSHES IT."

MAY 4, 1977 — THE PALLADIUM. THE THIRD AND FINAL NIGHT. ONE ATTENDEE'S OLDER BROTHER "WOUND UP TAKING OFF HIS CLOTHES" DURING THE SHOW. THERE WAS NO ENCORE — THE TERRAPIN STATION > PLAYING IN THE BAND > COMES A TIME > PLAYING IN THE BAND SUITE HAD ALREADY SAID EVERYTHING. THE COMES A TIME IN THE MIDDLE OF THE SUITE RIVALED THE MORE FAMOUS VERSION FROM BUFFALO FIVE DAYS LATER — FIFTEEN MINUTES OF PLAYING IN THE BAND JAM THAT ONE REVIEWER CALLED "SO RELAXED BUT ENTIRELY PRESENT AND ABSOLUTELY SOME OF THE MOST BEAUTIFUL AND INSPIRED IMPROVISATIONAL MUSIC OF THEIR ENTIRE CAREER." BY 1985, STUDIO 54'S STEVE RUBELL AND IAN SCHRAGER WOULD CONVERT THIS ROOM INTO A NIGHTCLUB WITH ARTWORK BY KEITH HARING AND JEAN-MICHEL BASQUIAT. NYU DEMOLISHED THE BUILDING IN 1998. NO TRACE REMAINS.

MAY 5, 1977 — NEW HAVEN COLISEUM. THE PARKING GARAGE SAT ON TOP OF THE ARENA — A QUARTER-MILE CORKSCREW RAMP TO REACH YOUR CAR ON THE ROOF — BECAUSE THE WATER TABLE WAS TOO HIGH FOR AN UNDERGROUND LOT. YALE'S ARCHITECTURAL HISTORIAN VINCENT SCULLY CALLED THE DESIGN "STRUCTURAL EXHIBITIONISM." THE ACOUSTICS WERE TERRIBLE. IT WOULD BE IMPLODED IN 2007 WITH TWO THOUSAND POUNDS OF EXPLOSIVES WHILE TWENTY THOUSAND PEOPLE WATCHED. THE PEGGY-O FROM THIS NIGHT IS WIDELY CONSIDERED THE BEST THE DEAD EVER PLAYED. ONE FAN: "EVERY SINGLE TIME I GET CHILLS AS JERRY UNLEASHES PURE BRILLIANCE DURING THE SOLO. THIS JAM IS WHAT MADE ME FALL HEAD OVER HEELS IN LOVE WITH THE GRATEFUL DEAD." AN EYEWITNESS "BLEW OFF AN EXAM, COPPED SOME FOUR-WAY ORANGE SUNSHINE, AND POPPED IT ON THE WAY DOWN." PART OF MAY 1977: GET SHOWN THE LIGHT.

MAY 7, 1977 — BOSTON GARDEN. THREE YEARS EARLIER, THE DEAD HAD PLAYED THIS SWELTERING, RAT-INFESTED BARN WITH NO AIR CONDITIONING AND A THIRTY-MINUTE JAM THAT "HINTED AT DARK STAR." NOW THEY WERE BACK WITH TWO DRUMMERS AND A PA SYSTEM THAT DIDN'T WEIGH SEVENTY-FIVE TONS. EQUIPMENT PROBLEMS DOGGED THE SHOW — HOT MICS CAPTURED THE BAND BITCHING BACKSTAGE — BUT THE MUSIC PUSHED THROUGH. THE MUSIC NEVER STOPPED IS WIDELY CALLED ONE OF THE BEST EVER. AN EYEWITNESS WHO'D BEEN SEEING THE DEAD SINCE 1976 DESCRIBED THE ERA'S SHOWS AS "FAMILY, EVEN TRIBAL AFFAIRS." THE NEXT NIGHT WAS CORNELL. PART OF MAY 1977: GET SHOWN THE LIGHT.

MAY 8, 1977 — BARTON HALL, CORNELL UNIVERSITY, ITHACA, NEW YORK. A MILITARY DRILL HALL BUILT IN 1917 FROM LOCAL LIMESTONE — NORMAN GOTHIC, WITH ARROW-SLIT WINDOWS AND TOWERS LIKE A CASTLE. FOR A LONG TIME, THE LARGEST UNPILLARED ROOM IN EXISTENCE. TONIGHT IT HELD ABOUT FIVE THOUSAND PEOPLE AT SIX DOLLARS AND FIFTY CENTS ADVANCE. IT WAS DRIZZLING AND COLD OUTSIDE. THE DOORS STAYED CLOSED TOO LONG AND ONLY ONE WAS OPENED, CREATING A CRUSH. HUNDREDS WERE ADMITTED WITHOUT TICKETS IN EXCHANGE FOR CREATIVE OFFERINGS — ONE PERSON WITH A GUITAR PICK, ANOTHER WITH A PEANUT BUTTER SANDWICH WRAPPED IN FOIL. INSIDE IT WAS STEAMING HOT AND PACKED BUT PEACEFUL. A FRESHMAN STOOD AT JERRY'S FEET: "MY JAW DROPPED, IN SHEER AWE." THIS IS THE MOST FAMOUS GRATEFUL DEAD SHOW OF ALL TIME. THE BETTY CANTOR-JACKSON SOUNDBOARD TAPE BECAME THE GATEWAY RECORDING FOR A GENERATION OF NEW FANS. THE LIBRARY OF CONGRESS ADDED IT TO THE NATIONAL RECORDING REGISTRY IN 2011. BOB WEIR SAID: "I CAN'T REMEMBER THAT SPECIFICALLY. IT DIDN'T STAND OUT FOR ME ON THAT TOUR. THE WHOLE TOUR WAS LIKE THAT FOR ME." WHEN THE CROWD EMERGED AT THE END OF THE SHOW, THEY WALKED INTO A SURPRISE MAY SNOWSTORM — SWIRLING WHITE FLAKES.

MAY 9, 1977 — WAR MEMORIAL AUDITORIUM, BUFFALO. "THE AUD." A WPA PROJECT FROM 1940, BUILT ON THE WATERFRONT WHERE THE BUFFALO RIVER MEETS LAKE ERIE. THE ACOUSTIC CYLINDERS HANGING FROM THE CEILING WOULD SWAY BACK AND FORTH WHEN THE CROWD ROARED DURING SABRES GAMES. BUFFALO WAS A PROUD, COLD, HARD-DRINKING, BLUE-COLLAR CITY WATCHING ITS ECONOMIC FOUNDATIONS CRUMBLE — BETHLEHEM STEEL ALREADY CUTTING THOUSANDS OF JOBS — THE QUINTESSENTIAL RUST BELT CITY BEFORE ANYONE HAD COINED THE TERM. THIS WAS THE DAY AFTER CORNELL AND MANY FANS RANK IT HIGHER. A FOURTEEN-YEAR-OLD AT HIS FIRST SHOW — DRAGGED BY HIS BROTHER — WENT ON TO SEE A HUNDRED AND FORTY-FIVE MORE. ANOTHER FAN STOOD IN THE THIRD ROW WITH HIS FIST RAISED "LIKE THE TWO BLACK ATHLETES IN MEXICO CITY, 1968" AND SWORE THAT GARCIA MADE EYE CONTACT MULTIPLE TIMES, LOOKING TO WEIR AND CHUCKLING: "JERRY ONCE SAID HE PICKED SOMEONE FROM THE FRONT OF THE AUDIENCE AND PLAYED THE SHOW TO THEM. THIS WAS MY SHOW." THE COMES A TIME NEARLY MADE PEOPLE CRY. THE HELP > SLIPKNOT! > FRANKLIN'S TOWER: "IF ANYONE DISAGREES THIS IS THE BEST EVER, PLEASE TELL US THE DATE OF A BETTER ONE." PART OF MAY 1977: GET SHOWN THE LIGHT.

MAY 11, 1977 — ST. PAUL CIVIC CENTER. THE ARENA HAD CLEAR ACRYLIC DASHER BOARDS ALL THE WAY TO THE ICE — A UNIQUE DESIGN THAT GAVE HOCKEY FANS AN UNOBSTRUCTED VIEW. THE MINNESOTA FIGHTING SAINTS HAD FOLDED THE YEAR BEFORE. IN 1977, A TEENAGED PRINCE, TERRY LEWIS, MORRIS DAY, AND ANDRE CYMONE WERE HANGING OUT AT A COMMUNITY CENTER CALLED THE WAY ON THE NORTH SIDE. THE MINNEAPOLIS SOUND WAS A FEW YEARS AWAY FROM EXPLODING. AN EYEWITNESS: "MY FIRST SHOW. WE PLAYED FRISBEE IN A PARK IN WEST ST. PAUL, HAD A BEER OR TWO, AND A FRIEND BROUGHT OUT A SUCRET'S BOX WITH SOME LITTLE PURPLE THINGS THAT WE ATE." DURING WHARF RAT, GARCIA BEGAN A FIVE-MINUTE SOLO INTRODUCTION WHERE THE REST OF THE BAND SIMPLY STOPPED PLAYING — "ONE OF THE MOST BRILLIANT TRANSITIONS EVER." PART OF THE MAY 1977 BOX SET.

MAY 13, 1977 — AUDITORIUM THEATRE, CHICAGO. CORNERSTONE LAID BY PRESIDENT GROVER CLEVELAND IN 1887. DEDICATED BY PRESIDENT BENJAMIN HARRISON IN 1889 WITH AN OPERA SINGER PERFORMING "HOME SWEET HOME." DESIGNED BY ADLER AND SULLIVAN — TWO OF THE MOST IMPORTANT ARCHITECTS IN AMERICAN HISTORY — WITH A YOUNG FRANK LLOYD WRIGHT WORKING ON THE INTERIOR. TWENTY-FOUR-KARAT GOLD LEAF ON THE CEILING ARCHES. ACOUSTICS THAT ADLER DESIGNED, IN AN ERA BEFORE SCIENTIFIC CALCULATIONS EXISTED, THAT ARE STILL INTERNATIONALLY RECOGNIZED AS PERFECT. THE FIRST HOME OF THE CHICAGO SYMPHONY ORCHESTRA. THE SHOW WAS SIMULCAST ON FM RADIO — A FRIEND RECORDED IT LIVE IN CHICAGO AND "WE TOOK IT ON A CAMPING TRIP OUT WEST AND WHEN ESTIMATED PROPHET PLAYED AS WE MADE OUR WAY THROUGH DEATH VALLEY, I DECIDED THIS BAND WAS WORTH LISTENING TO." ONE TOUR DIARIST'S ENTRY FOR THIS NIGHT: "LAST NIGHT I FORGOT MY CARDINAL RULE — NEVER EAT TWO OF ANYTHING. THEY WERE PURPLE, THEY WERE SMALL, AND I ATE THEM BOTH." BOBBY AND MICKEY OPENED WITH A PRANK THEY CALLED "DR. BEECHWOOD AND DR. SHOT." PART OF THE MAY 1977 BOX SET.

MAY 17, 1977 — MEMORIAL COLISEUM, UNIVERSITY OF ALABAMA, TUSCALOOSA. THE FIRST GRATEFUL DEAD SHOW IN ALABAMA. THE STUDENT GOVERNMENT HAD A BUDGET SURPLUS AND NEEDED TO SPEND IT. THE COLISEUM WAS BUILT IN 1968 — THREE YEARS AFTER GEORGE WALLACE HAD MADE HIS INFAMOUS "STAND IN THE SCHOOLHOUSE DOOR" AT THE BUILDING IT REPLACED. THIS WAS BEAR BRYANT'S ALABAMA, WHERE FOOTBALL WAS RELIGION AND THE CULTURE WAS ABOUT AS FAR FROM SAN FRANCISCO PSYCHEDELIA AS YOU COULD GET. FANS CALL IT "SOUTHERN CORNELL." THE MISSISSIPPI HALF-STEP IS REGULARLY CITED AS THE ALL-TIME BEST VERSION. THE JACK-A-ROE WAS THE ONE PHIL LESH SELECTED FOR HIS "PHIL ZONE" COMPILATION — AND IT "CALMED AN INFANT DAUGHTER BETTER THAN ANY PACIFIER." THE SCARLET > FIRE RIVALS CORNELL'S. ONE REVIEWER SUGGESTED THE HEAT CAME FROM "PERFORMING IN DONNA'S HOME STATE FOR THE FIRST TIME." THE SHOW WAS RESCHEDULED FROM APRIL — THE ORIGINAL POSTERS SURVIVE ON EBAY WITH THE WRONG DATE.

MAY 18, 1977 — FOX THEATRE, ATLANTA. THE CEILING IS A NIGHT SKY WITH NINETY-SIX CRYSTALLINE STARS — A THIRD OF THEM TWINKLE — AND PROJECTED CLOUDS THAT DRIFT SLOWLY OVERHEAD. THE SEVENTY-NINE-FOOT PROSCENIUM FRAMES A CURTAIN SEWN WITH SEQUINS AND RHINESTONES DEPICTING MOSQUES AND MOORISH RULERS. THERE IS AN EGYPTIAN BALLROOM MODELED AFTER THE TEMPLE OF RAMSES II AT KARNAK. THE LADIES' LOUNGE HAS A REPLICA OF KING TUT'S THRONE CHAIR. TUT'S TOMB HAD BEEN DISCOVERED JUST FIVE YEARS BEFORE THE BUILDING OPENED IN 1929. TWO YEARS EARLIER, THIS PLACE WAS SCHEDULED FOR DEMOLITION. THE LAST MOVIE SCREENING WAS "THE KLANSMEN" STARRING RICHARD BURTON AND O.J. SIMPSON. HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS PICKETED AND THE MAYOR DECLARED A MORATORIUM. THREE MILLION DOLLARS AND A NATIONAL HISTORIC LANDMARK DESIGNATION SAVED IT. NIGHT ONE OF TWO. THE EYES OF THE WORLD IS CONSIDERED BY MANY TO BE THE DEFINITIVE VERSION. DURING STELLA BLUE — POSSIBLY THE LONGEST EVER — THERE WAS "PIN-DROP SILENCE. NOT A PEEP." AN EYEWITNESS ON ACID SAW EVERYONE IN THE AUDIENCE AS DEMONS.

MAY 19, 1977 — FOX THEATRE, ATLANTA. NIGHT TWO. CONNOISSEURS WILL TELL YOU THIS IS THE BEST SHOW OF THE ENTIRE SPRING 1977 TOUR — "IF THIS SHOW HAD CIRCULATED INSTEAD OF CORNELL, IT WOULD BE CONSIDERED THE GREATEST." IT WAS BROADCAST ON WLIR RADIO AND CIRCULATED WIDELY FROM THAT SOURCE. THE SUGAREE CONTAINS THREE SEPARATE, DISTINCT JAMS — "ALL OF WHICH COULD STAND ALONE AND STILL BE AMAZING, BUT THEY ARE ALL PART OF ONE SONG." THE ROW JIMMY FEATURES JERRY SWITCHING BETWEEN NORMAL LEAD GUITAR AND SLIDE IN A WAY ONE FAN CALLED "UNFATHOMABLE." CHINA DOLL APPEARED FOR THE FIRST TIME SINCE 1974 — SOMEONE CRIED. THERE WAS NO ENCORE. AFTER EVERYTHING THE BAND AND THE AUDIENCE HAD JUST BEEN THROUGH, AN ENCORE WOULD HAVE BEEN AN IMPOSITION. "YOUR BRAIN CAN'T TAKE ANYMORE." RELEASED AS PART OF DICK'S PICKS VOLUME 29 — A SIX-CD SET, THE LONGEST IN THE ENTIRE SERIES AND THE ONLY ONE WITH MORE THAN FOUR DISCS.

MAY 21, 1977 — LAKELAND CIVIC CENTER, LAKELAND, FLORIDA. A SMALL CITY HALFWAY BETWEEN TAMPA AND ORLANDO, BUILT ON CITRUS, CATTLE, AND PHOSPHATE MINING — SEVENTY PERCENT OF THE PHOSPHATE PRODUCED IN THE ENTIRE UNITED STATES WAS EXTRACTED WITHIN TWENTY-FIVE MILES. IT IS ALSO HOME TO THE WORLD'S LARGEST COLLECTION OF FRANK LLOYD WRIGHT ARCHITECTURE — THIRTEEN BUILDINGS AT FLORIDA SOUTHERN COLLEGE, INCLUDING A CHAPEL WITH A SIXTY-FIVE-FOOT BELL TOWER. THE CIVIC CENTER WAS ONLY THREE YEARS OLD. ACE FREHLEY OF KISS HAD BEEN ELECTROCUTED ON THIS STAGE THE YEAR BEFORE — THE INCIDENT INSPIRED HIM TO WRITE "SHOCK ME." ONE ATTENDEE: "I REMEMBER CRYING BY THE TIME THEY GOT TO ME AND MY UNCLE." THE BAND HUDDLED AFTER BROWN-EYED WOMEN FOR WHAT SEEMED LIKE FOUR OR FIVE MINUTES BEFORE LAUNCHING INTO THE SECOND SET. RELEASED AS PART OF DICK'S PICKS VOLUME 29.

MAY 22, 1977 — THE SPORTATORIUM, PEMBROKE PINES, FLORIDA. A CONCRETE-AND-STEEL HANGAR IN THE SWAMPS OF SOUTH FLORIDA — NO AIR CONDITIONING WHEN IT OPENED, RAIN FALLING ON THE AUDIENCE AND STAGE DURING STORMS BECAUSE THE ROOF LEAKED. THE ONLY WAY IN WAS EIGHT MILES OF TWO-LANE ROAD THROUGH SWAMPLAND. BILLY JOEL CALLED IT "AN ACOUSTICAL NIGHTMARE." THE HEAD OF SECURITY SAID: "IT LEAKED, IT RATTLED, IT WAS A DUMP AND I AM SHOCKED IT DID NOT SINK IN THE MUCK." BUT BECAUSE IT WAS OUT IN THE MIDDLE OF NOWHERE, THERE WERE NO NOISE ORDINANCES. BANDS COULD PLAY AS LONG AS THEY WANTED. THE PARKING LOT PARTIES BECAME LEGENDARY. ELVIS HAD PLAYED HERE SIX MONTHS BEFORE HIS DEATH — TICKETS SOLD OUT IN TWO AND A HALF HOURS. THE CLOSING SEQUENCE — EYES OF THE WORLD > WHARF RAT > TERRAPIN STATION > MORNING DEW — IS FOUR CLOSERS FROM JERRY BACK TO BACK. PART OF THE SHOW WAS RELEASED AS DICK'S PICKS VOLUME 3.

MAY 25, 1977 — THE MOSQUE, RICHMOND. A SHRINERS TEMPLE — DOMES, MINARETS, ARABESQUE TILEWORK, AND A FORTY-SIX-HUNDRED-SEAT THEATER INSIDE A BUILDING THAT ALSO CONTAINED FORTY-TWO HOTEL ROOMS, A SWIMMING POOL, A THREE-LANE BOWLING ALLEY, FOUR BILLIARD ROOMS, AND A ROOF GARDEN. THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE CALLED IT "A WHIMSICAL EXAMPLE OF MOORISH REVIVAL ARCHITECTURE." THE ACOUSTICS WERE OUTSTANDING — "IF YOU'VE EVER BEEN TO THE MOSQUE, YOU KNOW IT WAS MADE FOR MUSIC." CAPACITY: THIRTY-FIVE HUNDRED. STAR WARS CAME OUT THIS SAME DAY. A SOPHOMORE AT THE UNIVERSITY OF RICHMOND GOT SEVENTH-ROW-CENTER RETURNED TICKETS AT THE BOX OFFICE ON THE DAY OF THE SHOW. ANOTHER ATTENDEE'S BROTHER GOT A JOB HELPING THE CREW SET UP EQUIPMENT. THE DEAD NEVER CAME BACK — THE VENUE WAS TOO SMALL FOR THEIR GROWING AUDIENCE. LOCAL MUSLIMS EVENTUALLY OBJECTED TO THE NAME; IT WAS RENAMED THE LANDMARK THEATER IN 1995. THIS WAS THE VERY FIRST DAVE'S PICKS — VOLUME 1.

MAY 26, 1977 — BALTIMORE CIVIC CENTER. BUILT ON THE SITE WHERE THE CONTINENTAL CONGRESS MET IN 1776. THE BEATLES PLAYED HERE IN 1964; JAMES BROWN WAS A REGULAR; LED ZEPPELIN SHOT BACKSTAGE FOOTAGE FOR "THE SONG REMAINS THE SAME." ELVIS PLAYED HERE IN MAY 1977 — LESS THAN THREE MONTHS BEFORE HIS DEATH — AND SOLD OUT IN TWO AND A HALF HOURS. THE DEAD CAME IN THE DAY AFTER STAR WARS AND THE MOSQUE. AN EYEWITNESS: "EVERYONE POLITELY STAYED IN THEIR SEATS THROUGH THE ENTIRE SHOW. THAT'S THE WAY A LOT OF THESE INDOOR SHOWS WERE OF THIS ERA DUE TO THE HEAVY-HANDED GOONS THEY HIRED FOR SECURITY." PHIL'S OPENING NOTE WAS DESCRIBED AS AN "INITIAL CANNON SHOT." DAVE'S PICKS VOLUME 41.

JUNE 8, 1977 — WINTERLAND ARENA. CLOSING THE SPRING TOUR AT HOME. AN EYEWITNESS DESCRIBED THEIR FIRST WINTERLAND SHOW AS ONE OF THE BEST LIVE MUSIC EXPERIENCES THEY'D HAD — "THE MUSIC FELT PHYSICAL ON THE FLOOR." THE SECOND SET JAM FROM EYES THROUGH NOT FADE AWAY "REACHES HEIGHTS MY POOR BRAIN CAN BARELY COMPREHEND."

JUNE 9, 1977 — WINTERLAND ARENA. THE FINAL NIGHT OF THE SPRING TOUR. EIGHTY-FIVE REVIEWS ON ARCHIVE.ORG — NEARLY UNANIMOUSLY FIVE STARS. EYEWITNESS: "JERRY WAS JUMPING AROUND ON STAGE, WINDMILLING FAT CHORDS ALA PETE TOWNSHEND." ANOTHER: "YES! JERRY LITERALLY DANCING ON STAGE. I ALWAYS TELL FOLKS THAT AND THEY DON'T BELIEVE ME." HELP > SLIPKNOT! > FRANKLIN'S TOWER: "THE NUMBER ONE HSF OF ALL TIME" — FRANKLIN'S RUNS THIRTY-ONE MINUTES. THE MORNING DEW PRODUCED "DIVINE INTERVENTION" AND "OUT-OF-BODY EXPERIENCES." ONE REVIEWER: "WHEN THE ALIENS GET HERE, AND FOR THOSE LIVING HERE, WE GIVE THEM THIS ON BEHALF OF US HUMANS." ORIGINALLY A THREE-NIGHT RUN — THE FOURTH SHOW WAS ADDED LATER, WHICH IS WHY THE 28TH IS THE NIGHT OFF. THE TERRAPIN STATION ALBUM WOULD BE RELEASED IN SEVEN WEEKS — GARCIA HAD ALREADY COMPLAINED THAT THE PRODUCER "PUT THE GRATEFUL DEAD IN A DRESS."

SEPTEMBER 3, 1977 — RACEWAY PARK, ENGLISHTOWN, NEW JERSEY. A QUARTER-MILE DRAG STRIP. A HUNDRED AND TWO THOUSAND TICKETS SOLD — ESTIMATES RAN AS HIGH AS A HUNDRED AND FIFTY THOUSAND ACTUALLY PRESENT. TWO PEOPLE DIED. TWO BABIES WERE BORN — ONE WOMAN WENT INTO LABOR DURING THE MARSHALL TUCKER BAND'S OPENING SET AND WAS EVACUATED BY HELICOPTER. A HELICOPTER CRASHED. JOHN SCHER HIRED STEVEDORES TO BRING HUNDREDS OF SHIPPING CONTAINERS TO ENCIRCLE THE AREA. THE NEIGHBORING TOWN OF MANALAPAN TRIED TO BLOCK THE CONCERT IN COURT, FEARING "MOTORCYCLE GANGS, DRUGS, AND VIOLENCE." A JUDGE REJECTED THE INJUNCTION. AUTHORITIES INSISTED THE HELLS ANGELS WOULD NOT BE PROVIDING SECURITY. PARKING WAS MILES AWAY. ONE ATTENDEE PARKED ON SOMEONE'S LAWN FOR TWENTY DOLLARS AND BROUGHT SIX TANKS OF NITROUS OXIDE FROM A HOSPITAL. IT WAS OVER A HUNDRED DEGREES. THE DEAD LEFT THE STAGE FOR SALT TABLETS. WEIR TOLD THE CROWD: "Y'ALL HANG OUT AND MAKE FRIENDS WITH THE BUGS." JERRY LEAPED OFF THE GROUND DURING HALF-STEP. DURING TRUCKIN', JERRY AND BOBBY WALKED "SIDE BY SIDE LIKE CHORUS GIRLS FROM RADIO CITY, STRUTTING AND STRUMMING ALL THE WAY ACROSS THE STAGE." FIRST TRUCKIN' IN OVER TWO YEARS. THE DEAD'S BIGGEST AUDIENCE SINCE WATKINS GLEN. RELEASED AS DICK'S PICKS VOLUME 15.

SEPTEMBER 28, 1977 — PARAMOUNT THEATRE, SEATTLE. A 1928 MOVIE PALACE BY RAPP AND RAPP — FOUR TIERS OF GRAND LOBBIES WITH FRENCH BAROQUE PLASTER, GOLD-LEAF MEDALLIONS, AND A WURLITZER ORGAN WITH NEARLY FOURTEEN HUNDRED PIPES AND HALF A MILLION SEPARATE PARTS. THE ORGAN SURVIVED BECAUSE THE PIPE CHAMBERS WERE BRICKED IN, MAKING REMOVAL IMPRACTICAL. THE "RETURN OF WOLF" SHOW — BOTH THE GUITAR AND THE SONG CAME BACK. JERRY HAD BEEN PLAYING TRAVIS BEAN GUITARS WITH ALUMINUM NECKS ALL SPRING, BUT TONIGHT WOLF WAS BACK WITH OVERWOUND PICKUPS AND A FATTER TONE. AN EYEWITNESS: "IT WAS LOUD, INTIMATE, WITH QUALITY SOUND, BACK IN THE DAYS WHEN JERRY WAS WEARING HIS DARK SUNGLASSES RAPIDLY PICKING HIS WAY THROUGH THE SCALES WHILE WEIR WAS JUMPING ALL OVER THE STAGE, AND LESH WAS PLUNKING AWAY WHERE EVERY BEAT WAS FELT REVERBERATING AGAINST YOUR STERNUM."

OCTOBER 1, 1977 — PARAMOUNT THEATRE, PORTLAND. SAME ARCHITECTS AS SEATTLE'S PARAMOUNT — RAPP AND RAPP — OPENED THE SAME MONTH IN 1928. ITALIAN ROCOCO REVIVAL, THREE THOUSAND SEATS, CRYSTAL CHANDELIERS, FURNISHINGS IMPORTED FROM FRENCH MUSEUMS. THE DEAD PLAYED TWO RAPP AND RAPP PARAMOUNTS ON CONSECUTIVE WEEKENDS. A CREW MEMBER WORKING FOR MONARCH ENTERTAINMENT: "THIS WAS MY SECOND STOP WORKING FOR THE BAND. THE BAND WAS PERFORMING WITH A RELAXED AUTHORITY THAT WAS THE KEY TO THEIR ARTISTRY. I COMMEND JOHN SCHER FOR HIRING CY KOCIS. KID CANDELARIO WAS THE CHAMPION BALL BUSTER." BEFORE BERTHA, PHIL TRIED TO CONVINCE JERRY TO PLAY GOOD LOVIN' — JERRY WOULDN'T HAVE IT. RELEASED AS DAVE'S PICKS VOLUME 45.

OCTOBER 2, 1977 — PARAMOUNT THEATRE, PORTLAND. NIGHT TWO. THE BAND OPENED WITH CASEY JONES — FIRST TIME SINCE 1974. GARCIA FORGOT THE WORDS TO THE THIRD VERSE. THEN THEY PLAYED DUPREE'S DIAMOND BLUES FOR THE FIRST TIME SINCE 1969 — JERRY FORGOT THOSE LYRICS TOO. ALSO ONLY THE FOURTH TRUCKIN' SINCE THE HIATUS. THREE COMEBACKS IN ONE NIGHT. "MY FIRST SHOW," AN ATTENDEE RECALLED. "WHEN THEY OPENED WITH THAT SLOPPY BUT HOT CASEY JONES, BAM — INSTANT DEADHEAD." ANOTHER FAN: "MY SECOND SHOW WAS EUGENE IN JANUARY '78, WHICH BLEW THIS ONE AWAY." THE SCARLET > FIRE TRANSITION HAS BEEN CALLED THE BEST OF THE YEAR — "LANGUID, MELODIOUS, ABSOLUTELY STUNNING." BEFORE A SHOW, SOMEONE HEARD JERRY SNORTING IN AN INTERVIEW — "YOU CAN LITERALLY HEAR HIS VOICE CHANGE." RELEASED AS DAVE'S PICKS VOLUME 45.

venue_name: University of New Mexico Johnson Gym
review: OCTOBER 7, 1977 — UNIVERSITY ARENA, ALBUQUERQUE. "THE PIT" — PLAYING FLOOR THIRTY-SEVEN FEET BELOW STREET LEVEL. ROUTE 66 STILL RAN THROUGH THE CITY. ONLY A PARTIAL SECOND-SET SOUNDBOARD SURVIVES — BUT THE WHARF RAT AND SUGAR MAGNOLIA ARE "AS GOOD AS IT GETS." THE TERRAPIN "GETS A LITTLE AWKWARD, LIKE JERRY IS NOT SURE WHAT TO SING NEXT" — FIFTEEN MINUTES, POSSIBLY THE LONGEST EVER.

OCTOBER 9, 1977 — MCNICHOLS SPORTS ARENA, DENVER. OPENED TWO YEARS EARLIER WITH A LAWRENCE WELK CONCERT — THE BIG-BAND KING WAS THE GO-TO ARENA CHRISTENER IN THE MID-SEVENTIES. ZZ TOP WAS THE FIRST ROCK BAND FIVE DAYS LATER. ZZ TOP WOULD ALSO PLAY THE VENUE'S FINAL EVENT IN 1999 — THEY BOTH OPENED AND CLOSED THE BUILDING. THE "RETURN OF WOLF" TOUR CONTINUED. ONE REVIEWER: "I DON'T KNOW IF THE GUITAR IS A LITTLE TOO HIGH AND HOT IN THE MIX, OR IF JERRY TRAMPED ON THE FUZZ PEDAL AND LEFT IT THERE, BUT HE SPORTS A SEARING TONE THROUGH MOST OF THIS SHOW." THE SUGAREE IS A STRONG CANDIDATE FOR BEST EVER. THE DEAD PLAYED MCNICHOLS THIRTEEN TIMES OVER EIGHTEEN YEARS. IT WAS DEMOLISHED IN 2000 FOR A PARKING LOT.

OCTOBER 11, 1977 — LLOYD NOBLE CENTER, UNIVERSITY OF OKLAHOMA, NORMAN. LIKE MCNICHOLS, IT OPENED WITH LAWRENCE WELK. THE ALLMAN BROTHERS FOLLOWED TWO DAYS LATER. THIS WAS FOOTBALL COUNTRY — OU SOONERS — AND THE DEAD WERE A LONG WAY FROM HOME. THE LAST HELP ON THE WAY > SLIPKNOT! > FRANKLIN'S TOWER WITH DONNA JEAN AND KEITH GODCHAUX. THE SUITE WOULDN'T RETURN FOR FIVE AND A HALF YEARS AND FOUR HUNDRED SHOWS. A FRONT-ROW EYEWITNESS AT HIS FIRST DEAD SHOW: "THE WHOLE BAND WAS WATCHING JERRY DURING SLIPKNOT AS HE SHOOK HIS HEAD NO... THEN WHEN HE FINALLY SAID YES AND THEY HIT THE FINAL PROGRESSION INTO FRANKLIN'S TOWER, I SERIOUSLY MELTED." THE CROWD WOULD NOT GET UP AND DANCE — THEY DIDN'T KNOW THE PROTOCOL. ANOTHER FAN WAS BORN WHILE THIS SHOW WAS HAPPENING, SEVEN HUNDRED MILES AWAY: "SHOT OUT OF THE WOMB LIKE PHIL'S OPENING BASS LINE ON HELP."

OCTOBER 28, 1977 — SOLDIERS AND SAILORS MEMORIAL HALL, KANSAS CITY. WWI MEMORIAL WITH CORINTHIAN COLUMNS AND BRONZE PLAQUES OF THE FALLEN. "THE AROUND AND AROUND IS ONE OF THE HOTTEST I CAN RECALL FROM ANY ERA WHATSOEVER. EVEN DONNA IS HITTING HER MARKS — DOWNRIGHT UNOBTRUSIVE, WHICH IS ABOUT THE HIGHEST PRAISE I EVER GIVE HER." THE CASEY JONES: "BOBBY MAKES THE SONG — CHECK OUT HIS FEEDBACK AND SUSTAIN AT THE END OF JERRY'S FIRST SOLO. IT'S BRILLIANT." PINK FLOYD HAD DEBUTED DARK SIDE OF THE MOON HERE IN 1972.

venue_name: Evans Field House, Northern Illinois U
review: OCTOBER 29, 1977 — EVANS FIELD HOUSE, DEKALB, ILLINOIS. BARBED-WIRE CAPITAL OF THE WORLD, DEEP IN CORN COUNTRY. SIX THOUSAND SEATS. ONE REVIEWER CALLED IT "THE GREATEST DEAD SHOW EVER." PHIL REPORTEDLY SAID IT WAS "THE HAPPIEST DAY OF HIS LIFE" — CONNECTED TO THE LET IT GROW, WHICH IS "ABSOLUTELY BONKERS." AN EYEWITNESS SUGGESTED THE SHOW TO DICK LATVALA FOR A DICK'S PICKS — DICK SAID THE RECORDINGS WEREN'T GOOD ENOUGH. ANOTHER DISCOVERED IT VIA A TAPE LEFT IN A CAR. RELEASED AS DAVE'S PICKS VOLUME 33.

OCTOBER 30, 1977 — ASSEMBLY HALL, INDIANA UNIVERSITY, BLOOMINGTON. DESIGNED BY THE SAME FIRM THAT BUILT THE JEFFERSON MEMORIAL — EGGERS AND HIGGINS OF NEW YORK. SEVENTEEN THOUSAND SEATS SUSPENDED FROM FIFTY-FOUR CABLES IN A SADDLE-TYPE ROOF SYSTEM BORROWED FROM BRIDGE CONSTRUCTION. MOST SEATING ON THE SIDES, NOT THE ENDS — INSPIRED BY A NORTH CAROLINA LIVESTOCK PAVILION. WHEN ELVIS PLAYED HERE, COLONEL TOM PARKER USED BLACK PLASTIC BAGS TO HIDE EMPTY BALCONY SEATS. BOBBY KNIGHT'S HOUSE. "THE CARNEGIE HALL OF BASKETBALL." THE DAY BEFORE, THE DEAD HAD PLAYED A SIX-THOUSAND-SEAT GYM IN A CORN TOWN. NOW THEY WERE IN A HUNDRED-AND-TWENTY-SEVEN-MILLION-POUND ARCHITECTURAL MARVEL. THE PEGGY-O FROM THIS NIGHT EARNED THE NICKNAME "PEGGY-ORGASM."

NOVEMBER 1, 1977 — COBO ARENA, DETROIT. IN 1963, AFTER THE LARGEST CIVIL RIGHTS MARCH IN AMERICAN HISTORY — A HUNDRED AND TWENTY-FIVE THOUSAND PEOPLE WALKING THROUGH DETROIT — MARTIN LUTHER KING JR. DELIVERED THE ORIGINAL VERSION OF "I HAVE A DREAM" IN THIS BUILDING. THE FAMOUS WASHINGTON VERSION CAME TWO MONTHS LATER. BERRY GORDY'S MOTOWN RECORDS PRESSED IT AS AN LP. DETROIT IN 1977 HAD LOST A THIRD OF ITS JOBS IN NINE YEARS. THE CHRYSLER BAILOUT WAS TWO YEARS AWAY. BUT THE MUSIC SCENE WAS FIERCE — TED NUGENT, ALICE COOPER, AND BOB SEGER HAD MADE COBO "THE HOME OF ARENA ROCK." DETROIT ALWAYS ROCKED HARDER WHEN THINGS WERE HARDEST. THE TRUCKIN' IN THE SECOND SET "PEAKS AND EVERYBODY SORT OF HITS THE PEAK AT DIFFERENT TIMES, SO JERRY MAKES THEM DO IT AGAIN." THE U.S. BLUES ENCORE WAS "SO POWERFUL IT SOUNDS LIKE THEY ARE READY TO DO ANOTHER SET."

NOVEMBER 4, 1977 — COTTERELL COURT, COLGATE UNIVERSITY, HAMILTON, NEW YORK. HOMECOMING WEEKEND. FEWER THAN A THOUSAND PEOPLE IN A SEVENTEEN-HUNDRED-SEAT GYM — THE SHOW HAPPENED BECAUSE RENAISSANCE CANCELLED. ANNOUNCED ONLY DAYS BEFOREHAND. AN EYEWITNESS WAS "LEANING ON THE STAGE" AND SAW "PHIL DANCING WITH DONNA DURING LET IT GROW." ANOTHER DROVE ALL NIGHT FROM PROVIDENCE AFTER HEARING ABOUT IT AT MIDNIGHT. "PROBABLY THE BEST SHOW I EVER SAW." THE EYES > ESTIMATED COMBINATION WAS PERFORMED HERE FOR THE FIRST TIME. THE JOHNNY B. GOODE ENCORE: "THE MOST INCREDIBLE ROCKIN' VERSION I'VE EVER HEARD." RELEASED AS DAVE'S PICKS VOLUME 12.

NOVEMBER 5, 1977 — COMMUNITY WAR MEMORIAL, ROCHESTER. THE ROLLING STONES' SEVEN-MINUTE CONCERT. GORILLA MONSOON'S CONSTRUCTION SITE. ONE EYEWITNESS: "BEST SHOW I EVER SAW OR HEARD" OUT OF SIXTY DEAD SHOWS. ANOTHER DESCRIBED DOORS COLLAPSING DURING ENTRY AND CROWD SURGE INJURIES — BUT THE PERFORMANCE WAS "BEAUTIFUL." A HITCHHIKER MADE IT TO THE SHOW AND BECAME A LIFER. THE SCARLET > FIRE LANDED HARD. DICK'S PICKS VOLUME 34 COVERS THE NIGHT BEFORE.

NOVEMBER 6, 1977 — BROOME COUNTY VETERANS MEMORIAL ARENA, BINGHAMTON. THE BUILDING WON A NATIONWIDE DESIGN COMPETITION — FIFTY-NINE FIRMS ENTERED — AND THE FIRST CONCERT WAS CHICAGO WITH BRUCE SPRINGSTEEN AS THE OPENER. BY LATE AFTERNOON THE PLACE WAS PACKED AND "IT WAS A LOVEFEST." THEN THE CROWD BLEW ALL THE DOORS OPEN. "EVERYONE JUST RAN IN. I SAW COPS IN THE CROWD BEING SPUN AROUND. NO TICKETS WERE EVER COLLECTED." SOMEONE HANDED WEIR A STREET SIGN THAT READ "STEPHEN ST." — THEY'D TAKEN IT FROM STEPHENS SQUARE. BILLY KREUTZMANN GRABBED THE SIGN AND MOUNTED IT ON TOP OF HIS CYMBAL STAND. THEN THEY PLAYED SAINT STEPHEN. THE HALF-STEP OPENER IS REGULARLY CALLED THE BEST EVER. THE TRUCKIN' ENDED WITH WHAT ONE REVIEWER DESCRIBED AS "A HEAVY METAL ENDING — MUCH MORE PINK FLOYD." STEVE PARISH ANNOUNCED AT THE ENCORE THAT "WE WERE A DAMN GOOD AUDIENCE" — YOU CAN HEAR IT ON THE TAPE. RELEASED AS DAVE'S PICKS VOLUME 25.

DECEMBER 27, 1977 — WINTERLAND ARENA. OPENING NIGHT OF THE YEAR-END RUN — FOUR SHOWS TO CLOSE THE GREATEST YEAR THE BAND WOULD EVER HAVE. THEY'D BEEN OFF FOR SEVEN WEEKS AND THERE WAS SOME RUST — BUT THE PEGGY-O IS ONE OF THE STRONGEST CANDIDATES FOR BEST EVER. THE FIRE ON THE MOUNTAIN WAS DESCRIBED AS "THE FUNKIEST, SOUPIEST, GOOPIEST FIRE YOU'LL EVER HEAR — LIKE PLAYED THROUGH MOLASSES AND FOGGY GLASSES. JERRY SINGING LIKE AARON NEVILLE." ONE REVIEWER NOTED THAT KEITH "BECOMES A THIRD DRUMMER PRETTY OFTEN" BY LATE 1977 — A SIGN OF THE DECLINE THAT WOULD DEEPEN OVER THE NEXT YEAR. ANOTHER WAS MORE BLUNT: "THEY WERE HIGH AS HELL ON A STIMULANT." RELEASED AS PART OF DICK'S PICKS VOLUME 10.

DECEMBER 29, 1977 — WINTERLAND ARENA. THE MIDDLE NIGHT OF THE NEW YEAR'S RUN. TWO EYEWITNESSES — INDEPENDENTLY — CALL THIS "MY FAVORITE SHOW THAT I HAVE PERSONALLY ATTENDED." ONE: "JERRY WAS JUMPING AROUND ON STAGE, WINDMILLING FAT CHORDS ALA PETE TOWNSHEND." THE OTHER: "YES! JERRY LITERALLY DANCING. I ALWAYS TELL FOLKS AND THEY DON'T BELIEVE ME. A VERY SPECIAL RAINY NIGHT WHEN EVERYTHING WAS JUST EXACTLY PERFECT." BOOKING TRIVIA: ORIGINALLY A THREE-NIGHT RUN. WHEN THEY ADDED THE FOURTH SHOW (THE 27TH), THEY NEEDED A NIGHT OFF (THE 28TH).

DECEMBER 30, 1977 — WINTERLAND ARENA. THE PENULTIMATE SHOW OF 1977. SAINT STEPHEN APPEARED — ONLY THE FOURTH SINCE THE SONG'S RETURN, AND EACH PERFORMANCE STILL FELT LIKE AN EVENT. THE ESTIMATED PROPHET THAT LED INTO EYES OF THE WORLD BUILT THROUGH THE KIND OF PATIENT, DYNAMIC TRANSITIONS THAT HAD DEFINED THE YEAR. "THIS WAS THE FIRST DAY OF THE REST OF MY LIFE," ONE FAN RECALLED. "CAUGHT TWO TICKETS AS A CHRISTMAS PRESENT. MY COLLEGE ROOMMATE FLEW UP FROM LA TO GO WITH ME. JERRY RIPPED THROUGH BIG RIVER INTO PEGGY-O AND I WAS HOOKED. BECAME A TAPE COLLECTOR THE FOLLOWING YEAR." ONE YEAR AND THREE HUNDRED AND SIXTY-FIVE DAYS OF MUSIC LATER — CLOSING NIGHT AT WINTERLAND, DECEMBER 31, 1978 — THE BUILDING ITSELF WOULD CLOSE FOREVER. RELEASED AS PART OF DICK'S PICKS VOLUME 10.

APRIL 22, 1979 — SPARTAN STADIUM, SAN JOSE STATE UNIVERSITY. AN OUTDOOR STADIUM NEXT TO THE SAN JOSE AIRPORT. IT WAS RAINING. THE GREG KIHN BAND AND CHARLIE DANIELS OPENED. THIS WAS BRENT MYDLAND'S FIRST SHOW WITH THE GRATEFUL DEAD. HE WAS TWENTY-SIX YEARS OLD. HE'D BEEN PLAYING COCKTAIL LOUNGES AND JAZZ BARS SINCE FOURTEEN, HAD A HIT SINGLE WITH THE BAND SILVER ("WHAM BAM," NUMBER SIXTEEN, 1976), AND HAD BEEN PLAYING ORGAN IN BOB WEIR'S SOLO BAND WHEN GARCIA HEARD HIM IN PORTLAND AND SAID: "THIS GUY MIGHT WORK." HE'D REHEARSED WITH THE DEAD FOR ABOUT A MONTH. GARCIA FORGOT TO INTRODUCE HIM TO THE AUDIENCE. DURING NEW MINGLEWOOD BLUES, BRENT "UNCORKED A SOLO" THAT ANNOUNCED HE WAS NOT KEITH GODCHAUX'S CAUTIOUS REPLACEMENT BUT SOMETHING ENTIRELY DIFFERENT — SOULFUL, GRITTY, AGGRESSIVE. ONE REVIEWER: "WHAT'S SHOCKING IS JUST HOW WELL BRENT DOES. IT DOESN'T SOUND THAT MUCH DIFFERENT FROM WHERE HE'D BE SIX MONTHS FROM NOW." ANOTHER: "THERE WAS NO ONE IN THE DEAD WHO WAS AS CONSISTENTLY ON, BOTH EMOTIONALLY AND MUSICALLY, AS BRENT WAS." HE BROUGHT A HAMMOND B-3 ORGAN WITH TEN MODIFIED LESLIE SPEAKERS AND A SEQUENTIAL CIRCUITS PROPHET-5 SYNTHESIZER THE BAND BOUGHT HIM — CUTTING EDGE FOR 1979. AN ATTENDEE MADE ROSE WREATHS AND BOUGHT A COUNTERFEIT TICKET; BILL GRAHAM LET THEM IN ANYWAY. SOMEONE TOOK "A GREAT SHOT OF BOBBY WITH RAINDROPS ON HIS DENIM SHIRT DURING LOOKS LIKE RAIN." BRENT WOULD STAY FOR ELEVEN YEARS — LONGER THAN ANY OTHER DEAD KEYBOARDIST.

MAY 5, 1979 — BALTIMORE CIVIC CENTER. BRENT MYDLAND HAS BEEN IN THE BAND FOR THIRTEEN SHOWS. HE IS TWENTY-SIX YEARS OLD AND PLAYING LIKE HE HAS SOMETHING TO PROVE. THE SUGAREE RUNS FOURTEEN MINUTES — GARCIA STRETCHING INTO CORNERS HE HADN'T VISITED SINCE THE EUROPE TOUR. THE OTHER ONE IN THE SECOND SET REACHES BACK TO THE MOST AGGRESSIVE 1973 VERSIONS, WITH TIGHT SPIRALS AND ABRUPT TEMPO SHIFTS THAT FEEL DANGEROUS. THREE MILE ISLAND WAS FIVE WEEKS AGO AND NINETY MILES SOUTH OF HERE — THE NEAR-MELTDOWN STILL IN THE NEWS, THE PARKING LOT FULL OF BUMPER STICKERS ABOUT IT. THE SCARLET BEGONIAS TRANSITIONS INTO FIRE ON THE MOUNTAIN WITH AN UNUSUALLY SLOW, GRACEFUL BRIDGE — THE BAND FLOATING BETWEEN THE TWO SONGS INSTEAD OF CHARGING THROUGH. DANCIN' IN THE STREET CLOSES THE FIRST SET AT A SPRINT, BUILDING TO A FAST-PACED CLIMAX. THE RECORDING IS A PRISTINE AUDIENCE TAPE BY JOANI WALKER — ONE OF THE BEST-SOUNDING AUDIENCE CAPTURES OF THE YEAR.

AUGUST 4, 1979 — OAKLAND AUDITORIUM ARENA. THREE DEBUTS IN ONE NIGHT. ALTHEA — WHICH STEREOGUM WOULD LATER RANK AS THE FIFTH GREATEST DEAD SONG EVER WRITTEN — SOUNDS FULLY FORMED ON ITS FIRST OUTING, AS IF THE BAND HAS BEEN PLAYING IT FOR YEARS. LOST SAILOR APPEARS WITHOUT SAINT OF CIRCUMSTANCE, WHICH WON'T DEBUT FOR ANOTHER MONTH. AND GARCIA IS PLAYING A NEW GUITAR: TIGER, BUILT BY DOUG IRWIN OVER SIX YEARS AND TWO THOUSAND HOURS OF WORK, THIRTEEN AND A HALF POUNDS OF COCOBOLO AND MAPLE WITH AN ONBOARD PREAMP. HE WILL PLAY IT EXCLUSIVELY FOR THE NEXT ELEVEN YEARS. THIS IS ALSO THE DEAD'S FIRST SHOW AT OAKLAND AUDITORIUM SINCE BILL GRAHAM BEGAN BOOKING IT AS WINTERLAND'S REPLACEMENT — SEVEN THOUSAND SEATS, FREE PARKING AT LANEY COLLEGE, AND A GENERAL ADMISSION FLOOR THAT WOULD BECOME THEIR HOME COURT FOR THE ENTIRE BRENT ERA. THE PLAYING IN THE BAND IS TWENTY MINUTES OF PSYCHEDELIC FURY, FAR MORE ADVENTUROUS THAN ANYONE EXPECTED FROM 1979.

SEPTEMBER 2, 1979 — AUGUSTA CIVIC CENTER, MAINE. LABOR DAY WEEKEND. THE NIGHT BEFORE, IN ROCHESTER, FIVE HUNDRED NEIGHBORS HAD SIGNED A PETITION TO STOP THE SHOW AND A STATE SUPREME COURT JUDGE HAD TO INTERVENE. AUGUSTA IS CALMER — AN INDOOR ARENA, SMALLER CROWD, THE BAND LOOSE AND WARM. THE TERRAPIN STATION IS GENTLE, ALMOST PASTORAL. STAGGER LEE IS ONE OF THE STANDOUT EARLY VERSIONS — GARCIA LEANING INTO THE NARRATIVE WITH RELISH. BRENT HAS BEEN IN THE BAND FOR FOUR MONTHS NOW AND THE CHEMISTRY IS SETTLING. THE TIGER GUITAR HAS BEEN IN GARCIA'S HANDS FOR EXACTLY FOUR WEEKS. THE FIRST SET IS A TEN-SONG RAMBLE THROUGH THE CATALOG — MAMA TRIED, MEXICALI BLUES, CASSIDY, ROW JIMMY, JACK-A-ROE — WITH THE MUSIC NEVER STOPPED AS A PROPULSIVE CLOSER. THE SECOND SET BUILDS FROM SAMSON AND DELILAH THROUGH A LONG TERRAPIN AND LET IT GROW JAM INTO DRUMS AND SPACE, EMERGING WITH A STELLA BLUE THAT JUSTIFIES THE WHOLE EVENING.

OCTOBER 25, 1979 — VETERANS MEMORIAL COLISEUM, NEW HAVEN. SECOND NIGHT OF THE FALL TOUR. THE SHAKEDOWN STREET IS ONE OF THE BEST THE DEAD EVER PLAYED — A LONG, BOUNCY, IMPOSSIBLY FUNKY VERSION WHERE PHIL DRIVES THE OPENING AS THE VOCALS FADE INTO A UNIVERSE OF GROOVE, BRENT AND GARCIA TRADING LINES OVER THE TOP. IT WOULD LATER OPEN DISC TWO OF ROAD TRIPS VOLUME 1, NUMBER 1, THE INAUGURAL RELEASE IN THAT SERIES. THE ESTIMATED PROPHET FLOWS INTO EYES OF THE WORLD AND THEN INTO SPACE AND DRUMS — THE ENTIRE SECOND SET A CONTINUOUS ARC WITH NO HARD STOPS. THE STELLA BLUE THAT EMERGES FROM THE OTHER SIDE IS DEVASTATING. THE FIRST SET IS SOLID BUT CONVENTIONAL — BROWN EYED WOMEN, TENNESSEE JED, DEAL AS THE CLOSER. IT'S THE SECOND SET THAT MAKES THIS TAPE ESSENTIAL. BRENT'S ORGAN WORK ON THE SHAKEDOWN IS PARTICULARLY NOTABLE — HE IS FINDING HIS ROLE NOT AS A REPLACEMENT FOR KEITH BUT AS SOMETHING THE BAND HAS NEVER HAD BEFORE.

OCTOBER 27, 1979 — CAPE COD COLISEUM, SOUTH YARMOUTH, MASSACHUSETTS. A FORTY-SIX-THOUSAND-SQUARE-FOOT CONCRETE ARENA IN A BEACH TOWN ON CAPE COD — FIVE THOUSAND SEATS, BUILT IN 1972 FOR ONE AND A HALF MILLION DOLLARS. AEROSMITH, THE CLASH, AND THE J. GEILS BAND ALL PLAYED HERE. LATER SOLD TO VINCE MCMAHON FOR WWF EVENTS, THEN TO CHRISTMAS TREE SHOPS FOR USE AS A WAREHOUSE. WIDELY CONSIDERED THE BEST GRATEFUL DEAD SHOW OF 1979. THE SECOND SET "DEBATABLY EARNS THE TITLE OF THE BEST SET OF THE TOUR, IF NOT THE ENTIRE YEAR." THE FOURTEEN-MINUTE DANCIN' IN THE STREETS "LIFTS THE CROWD" WITH JAMMING THAT "SWIRLS LIKE A SEA OF CITY LIGHTS SEEN FROM ABOVE." THE SEVENTEEN-MINUTE FRANKLIN'S TOWER BUILDS DRAMATICALLY. HE'S GONE EVOLVES INTO TERRITORY WHERE "SPINNING, GALAXY-HUGE, WAGON WHEELS OF LIGHT" APPEAR. THE OTHER ONE FEATURES PHIL'S BASS "OVERWHELMING IN POWER." INCLUDED IN THE 30 TRIPS AROUND THE SUN BOX SET.

OCTOBER 31, 1979 — NASSAU COLISEUM, LONG ISLAND. HALLOWEEN. THE DEAD OPEN WITH CHINA CAT SUNFLOWER INTO I KNOW YOU RIDER — A DECLARATION OF INTENT. ALTHEA APPEARS IN THE FIRST SET, STILL LESS THAN THREE MONTHS OLD AND ALREADY SOUNDING LIKE A STANDARD. LOST SAILOR INTO SAINT OF CIRCUMSTANCE CLOSES THE SET — THE PAIRING NOW LOCKED IN AS INSEPARABLE. THE SECOND-SET SHAKEDOWN STREET IS ENORMOUS — THIRTY-NINE HEADYVERSION VOTES, ONE OF THE HIGHEST-RATED VERSIONS FROM ANY YEAR. THE ESTIMATED PROPHET INTO EYES OF THE WORLD IS LOADED WITH HOT JAMMING. JOHNNY B. GOODE FOR THE ENCORE — A HALLOWEEN PARTY CLOSER. THIS IS THE FIRST OF THREE NIGHTS AT NASSAU, AND THE BAND IS TREATING IT LIKE AN EVENT. CHINA CAT AND ALTHEA FROM THIS SHOW APPEAR ON THE ROAD TRIPS VOLUME 1, NUMBER 1 BONUS DISC. ONE WEEK FROM TONIGHT, IRANIAN STUDENTS WILL STORM THE AMERICAN EMBASSY IN TEHRAN.

NOVEMBER 1, 1979 — NASSAU COLISEUM. NIGHT TWO. ELEVEN SONGS IN THE FIRST SET — THE BAND IS GENEROUS. EASY TO LOVE YOU, BRENT'S FIRST ORIGINAL COMPOSITION WITH THE DEAD, SITS COMFORTABLY BETWEEN LOOKS LIKE RAIN AND DON'T EASE ME IN. HE WROTE IT WITH JOHN PERRY BARLOW, THE SAME LYRICIST WHO'D WRITTEN CASSIDY AND MEXICALI BLUES FOR WEIR. THE SCARLET BEGONIAS INTO FIRE ON THE MOUNTAIN WOULD LATER SURFACE AS A HIDDEN TRACK ON DICK'S PICKS VOLUME 13 — THE ONLY MATERIAL FROM THIS SHOW TO RECEIVE AN OFFICIAL RELEASE. THE TERRAPIN STATION IN THE SECOND SET IS MUSCULAR, AND THE PLAYING IN THE BAND THAT FOLLOWS IT STRETCHES INTO DRUMS AND SPACE BEFORE RESOLVING THROUGH BLACK PETER INTO A SCORCHING GOOD LOVIN'. NASSAU IS A FIFTEEN-THOUSAND-SEAT LONG ISLAND ARENA THE DEAD WOULD PLAY FORTY-TWO TIMES BETWEEN 1973 AND 1994. BY NIGHT TWO THE BAND IS SETTLED IN AND SWINGING.

NOVEMBER 2, 1979 — NASSAU COLISEUM. NIGHT THREE. THE OPENER IS A MISSISSIPPI HALF-STEP THAT SEEMS TO LAST A LIFETIME — EXTENSIVE JAMMING BEFORE THE LYRICS ARRIVE — AND THEN IT DISSOLVES DIRECTLY INTO FRANKLIN'S TOWER. THE CROWD ERUPTS. LAZY LIGHTNING INTO SUPPLICATION IS TRULY IMPRESSIVE, THE BAND LOCKED INTO A GROOVE THAT TIGHTENS WITH EACH PASS. THE SECOND SET OPENS WITH I NEED A MIRACLE INTO BERTHA, THEN LOST SAILOR INTO SAINT OF CIRCUMSTANCE BUILDS INTO AN EXTENDED JAM THROUGH DRUMS AND SPACE. NOT FADE AWAY EMERGES FROM THE OTHER SIDE IN EVOLVED FORM — LOOSER, MORE EXPLORATORY THAN THE STANDARD ARRANGEMENT. THE CASEY JONES ENCORE IS BRENT MYDLAND'S FIRST TIME PLAYING THE SONG — A SMALL MILESTONE MARKING HOW COMPLETELY THE NEW LINEUP HAS ABSORBED THE CATALOG. THIS IS THE FINAL NIGHT OF THE NASSAU HALLOWEEN RUN. TWO DAYS OFF FOLLOW BEFORE PROVIDENCE. THE SHOW LOOKS ORDINARY ON PAPER AND SMOKES IN PRACTICE. THE JAMS ARE WHERE THE MAGIC LIVES.

NOVEMBER 6, 1979 — THE SPECTRUM, PHILADELPHIA. NIGHT TWO. THE IRAN HOSTAGE CRISIS IS TWO DAYS OLD. ALABAMA GETAWAY OPENS THE SHOW — THE SONG DEBUTED JUST TWO DAYS EARLIER IN PROVIDENCE, SO THIS IS ONE OF ITS VERY FIRST PERFORMANCES. THE SECOND SET IS ESSENTIALLY A SINGLE CONTINUOUS SUITE: TERRAPIN STATION FLOWS INTO PLAYING IN THE BAND, WHICH FLOWS INTO DRUMS AND SPACE, WHICH RESOLVES THROUGH BLACK PETER INTO GOOD LOVIN'. SIX SONGS, NO BREAKS, ONE UNINTERRUPTED ARC. BRENT'S B-3 ORGAN MAKES IT FEEL LIKE A PSYCHEDELIC CHURCH REVIVAL. THE SPECTRUM — WHOSE ROOF BLEW OFF TWICE IN ITS FIRST FIVE MONTHS — HOSTED THE DEAD FIFTY-THREE TIMES, MORE THAN ANY OTHER MUSICAL ACT. THIS SHOW WAS RELEASED TWICE: FOUR TRACKS ON ROAD TRIPS VOLUME 1, NUMBER 1, AND THE COMPLETE PERFORMANCE AS A ROAD TRIPS DIGITAL DOWNLOAD. NINETY MILES FROM THREE MILE ISLAND, FIVE WEEKS BEFORE THE OAKLAND NEW YEAR'S RUN.

NOVEMBER 9, 1979 — COMMUNITY WAR MEMORIAL AUDITORIUM, BUFFALO. SHOW TWELVE OF FOURTEEN ON THE FALL TOUR, AND ARGUABLY THE BEST OF THE RUN. THE DANCIN' IN THE STREET IS CRUSHINGLY FAST — AN ALMOST RECKLESS TEMPO THAT THE BAND RIDES WITHOUT FALLING OFF — AND IT POURS DIRECTLY INTO FRANKLIN'S TOWER, BOTH SONGS AMONG THE HIGHEST-RATED VERSIONS ON HEADYVERSION. THE THEY LOVE EACH OTHER CONTAINS WHAT ONE REVIEWER CALLED A DEFINITIVE GARCIA SOLO — THE KIND OF PASSAGE WHERE EVERYTHING HE KNOWS ABOUT THE INSTRUMENT CONVERGES IN A SINGLE TAKE. THE HE'S GONE FLOWS INTO A JAM THAT QUOTES GLORIA BEFORE DISSOLVING INTO DRUMS. NINE TRACKS FROM THIS SHOW APPEAR ON ROAD TRIPS VOLUME 1, NUMBER 1 — MORE THAN ANY OTHER SHOW IN THE RELEASE. THE ROLLING STONES ONCE PLAYED THIS ARENA FOR SEVEN MINUTES BEFORE THE CROWD RUSHED THE STAGE AND POLICE SHUT IT DOWN. THE DEAD GET A FULL EVENING AND MAKE THE MOST OF IT.

NOVEMBER 10, 1979 — CRISLER ARENA, ANN ARBOR. THE UNIVERSITY OF MICHIGAN'S FOURTEEN-THOUSAND-SEAT BASKETBALL ARENA. LAST SHOW OF THE MAIN FALL TOUR LEG — THIRTEEN DAYS OFF BEFORE SAN DIEGO. GARCIA PLAYS ELEVEN SONGS IN THE FIRST SET, INCLUDING HIGH TIME, A DEEPLY EMOTIONAL BALLAD HE RARELY PERFORMED. THE PASSENGER IS A JAM ANTHEM VERSION — GARCIA'S FINAL SOLO DESCRIBED AS HAVING HURRICANE-FORCE INTENSITY. SHIP OF FOOLS RUNS ALMOST NINE MINUTES AT AN UNUSUALLY SLOW PACE, GARCIA STAYING CLOSE TO THE MELODY IN A QUIET, CONTEMPLATIVE READING. THE SECOND SET OPENS WITH ALABAMA GETAWAY INTO THE PROMISED LAND — JUST THE SIXTH PERFORMANCE OF ALABAMA GETAWAY — THEN TERRAPIN INTO PLAYING IN THE BAND INTO DRUMS AND OUT THROUGH NOT FADE AWAY. ONE MORE SATURDAY NIGHT FOR THE ENCORE, BECAUSE IT IS. ROAD TRIPS VOLUME 1, NUMBER 1 INCLUDES THE ALABAMA GETAWAY, PROMISED LAND, AND THE PASSENGER. AVERAGE FOR FALL 1979, WHICH REPRESENTS A HIGH BAR.

DECEMBER 26, 1979 — OAKLAND AUDITORIUM ARENA. OPENING NIGHT OF THE FIRST-EVER OAKLAND NEW YEAR'S RUN — A SEVA FOUNDATION BENEFIT FOR WAVY GRAVY'S SIGHT-RESTORATION CHARITY. WINTERLAND CLOSED A YEAR AGO AND THIS SEVEN-THOUSAND-SEAT ART DECO BUILDING ON LAKE MERRITT IS NOW THE DEAD'S HOME COURT. FREE PARKING AT LANEY COLLEGE ACROSS THE STREET. OUT-OF-TOWN DEADHEADS CAMPING ON THE LAWN OUTSIDE, SELLING BURRITOS AND TIE-DYE FROM BLANKETS ON THE GROUND — THE INFORMAL MARKETPLACE THAT BOB BARSOTTI ALLOWED TO FLOURISH DURING THIS RUN WOULD BECOME KNOWN AS SHAKEDOWN STREET AND DEFINE DEAD TOUR CULTURE FOR THE NEXT SIXTEEN YEARS. THE SHAKEDOWN STREET ENCORE IS RANKED SEVENTH ALL-TIME ON HEADYVERSION WITH FIFTY-TWO VOTES — ONE OF THE DEFINITIVE VERSIONS OF THE SONG. THE ENTIRE SHOW WAS RELEASED AS DICK'S PICKS VOLUME 5.

DECEMBER 28, 1979 — OAKLAND AUDITORIUM ARENA. NIGHT THREE OF THE NEW YEAR'S RUN. SATURDAY NIGHT — DOUBLE ENCORE OF CASEY JONES AND ONE MORE SATURDAY NIGHT. THE IT'S ALL OVER NOW IS RANKED NUMBER ONE ALL-TIME ON HEADYVERSION WITH TWENTY-SEVEN VOTES — THE DEFINITIVE VERSION OF A SONG THE DEAD ONLY PLAYED SPORADICALLY. HIGH TIME APPEARS IN THE FIRST SET, A WORKINGMAN'S DEAD BALLAD SO RARELY PERFORMED THAT ITS PRESENCE SIGNALS GARCIA IN A PARTICULAR MOOD — REFLECTIVE, UNHURRIED, WILLING TO SIT INSIDE A SONG INSTEAD OF STRETCHING IT. THE PLAYING IN THE BAND IN THE SECOND SET OPENS INTO DRUMS AND SPACE AND RESOLVES THROUGH UNCLE JOHN'S BAND — THE KIND OF CONTINUOUS SEQUENCE WHERE THE VENUE AND THE CLOCK DISAPPEAR. THE ENTIRE SHOW WAS RELEASED AS ROAD TRIPS VOLUME 3, NUMBER 1.

DECEMBER 30, 1979 — OAKLAND AUDITORIUM ARENA. NIGHT FIVE — PENULTIMATE BEFORE NEW YEAR'S EVE. THE DOUBLE ENCORE IS EMOTIONAL WHIPLASH: DON'T EASE ME IN, A ROWDY JUG-BAND ROMP, FOLLOWED IMMEDIATELY BY BROKEDOWN PALACE, ONE OF GARCIA'S MOST DEVASTATING BALLADS. THE TRANSITION FROM PARTY TO EULOGY IN TWO SONGS. THE SCARLET BEGONIAS INTO FIRE ON THE MOUNTAIN ANCHORS THE SECOND SET. LAZY LIGHTNING INTO SUPPLICATION IS A RARITY BY THIS ERA — THE PAIRING WAS A 1976-77 STAPLE BUT APPEARS LESS AND LESS FREQUENTLY. WHEN IT SHOWS UP, IT LANDS HARD. WHARF RAT EMERGES FROM SPACE WITH THE WEIGHT IT ALWAYS CARRIES — A SONG ABOUT A DRUNK ON A PARK BENCH THAT GARCIA SINGS LIKE HE'S LIVED EVERY LINE. PARTS OF THIS SHOW APPEAR ON THE ROAD TRIPS VOLUME 3, NUMBER 1 BONUS DISC.

DECEMBER 31, 1979 — OAKLAND AUDITORIUM ARENA. NEW YEAR'S EVE. THREE SETS. THE FLYING KARAMAZOV BROTHERS — A SANTA CRUZ JUGGLING TROUPE, NEITHER BROTHERS NOR RUSSIAN — OPEN THE SHOW, HURLING CHAMPAGNE BOTTLES AND MEAT CLEAVERS AND BURNING TORCHES ACROSS A THIRTY-FOOT SPREAD. AT MIDNIGHT, GARCIA WALKS CASUALLY FROM STAGE LEFT THROUGH THE SEMICIRCLE OF FLYING OBJECTS WITHOUT BREAKING STRIDE. THE JUGGLERS NEVER DROP A THING. THE CROWD LOSES ITS MIND. QUICKSILVER MESSENGER SERVICE GUITARIST JOHN CIPOLLINA SITS IN FOR THE FINAL THREE SONGS OF THE THIRD SET — NOT FADE AWAY, STELLA BLUE, AND SUNSHINE DAYDREAM. THE SET LIST IS A GREATEST-HITS VICTORY LAP: CHINA CAT INTO RIDER, ALTHEA, SHIP OF FOOLS, UNCLE JOHN'S BAND, LOST SAILOR INTO SAINT OF CIRCUMSTANCE. THIS IS THE FIRST OAKLAND NEW YEAR'S EVE — THE FORMAT THAT WOULD DEFINE THE DEAD'S CALENDAR FOR THE NEXT TWELVE YEARS.

JANUARY 13, 1980 — OAKLAND-ALAMEDA COUNTY COLISEUM ARENA. NOT THE AUDITORIUM — THE BIG BUILDING. A CAMBODIAN REFUGEE BENEFIT ORGANIZED BY JOAN BAEZ AND BILL GRAHAM. THE DEAD HEADLINE ALONGSIDE THE BEACH BOYS, JEFFERSON STARSHIP, AND CARLOS SANTANA. THE SET IS SHORT — ROUGHLY NINETY MINUTES, ABBREVIATED FOR THE MULTI-ARTIST FORMAT — BUT LOADED. CARLOS SANTANA AND JOHN CIPOLLINA JOIN FOR NOT FADE AWAY AND SUGAR MAGNOLIA. GREG ERRICO, THE ORIGINAL SLY AND THE FAMILY STONE DRUMMER, SITS IN ON U.S. BLUES. THE SHOW IS BROADCAST LIVE ON KSAN-FM — THE LAST LIVE GRATEFUL DEAD BROADCAST ON THE STATION THAT HAD BEEN THE BAY AREA'S PIONEERING UNDERGROUND ROCK OUTLET SINCE THE SIXTIES. STRUCTURED AS A TELETHON WITH PLEDGE-DRIVE PHONE LINES. THIS IS NOT A NORMAL DEAD SHOW — IT'S A BENEFIT, A BROADCAST, AND A GATHERING OF SAN FRANCISCO ROCK ROYALTY ON ONE STAGE.

MARCH 30, 1980 — CAPITOL THEATRE, PASSAIC. THE FORMER PORN THEATER — THIRTY-ONE HUNDRED SEATS, PROMOTER JOHN SCHER RUNNING THE ROOM. FAR FROM ME, BRENT MYDLAND'S FIRST ORIGINAL COMPOSITION, SITS IN THE MIDDLE OF AN ELEVEN-SONG FIRST SET. LOST SAILOR INTO SAINT OF CIRCUMSTANCE CLOSES IT — UNUSUAL POSITIONING FOR A SET-ONE CLOSER. JOHN BELUSHI SITS IN ON THE U.S. BLUES ENCORE. HE IS AT THE HEIGHT OF HIS FAME — THE BLUES BROTHERS MOVIE COMES OUT IN TWO MONTHS — AND HE IS A GENUINE DEAD FAN WHO OPENED FOR THEM AT WINTERLAND'S FINAL SHOW ON NEW YEAR'S EVE 1978. HE WILL BE DEAD IN LESS THAN TWO YEARS. TOMORROW NIGHT AT THIS SAME VENUE, THE BAND WILL DEBUT FEEL LIKE A STRANGER. TONIGHT THEY DON'T KNOW THAT YET. THE SCARLET INTO FIRE IN THE SECOND SET IS SOLID, THE ESTIMATED INTO EYES SPACIOUS AND EXPLORATORY.

MAY 15, 1980 — NASSAU COLISEUM, LONG ISLAND. SEVENTEEN DAYS AFTER GO TO HEAVEN'S RELEASE — THE WHITE-SUIT ERA, THE BAND'S LAST STUDIO ALBUM FOR SEVEN YEARS. THE LAZY LIGHTNING INTO SUPPLICATION IS RANKED SIXTH ALL-TIME ON HEADYVERSION WITH FORTY VOTES — THE KIND OF LOCKED-IN, PROPULSIVE VERSION WHERE EVERY MUSICIAN IS CHASING THE SAME BEAT AND NOBODY BLINKS. THE SECOND SET IS A TEN-SONG UNBROKEN CHAIN: LOST SAILOR INTO SAINT OF CIRCUMSTANCE INTO TERRAPIN STATION INTO PLAYING IN THE BAND INTO UNCLE JOHN'S BAND INTO DRUMS INTO SPACE INTO NOT FADE AWAY INTO GOIN' DOWN THE ROAD FEELING BAD INTO GOOD LOVIN'. NO STOPS. NO BREAKS. ONE CONTINUOUS ARC FROM FIRST NOTE TO LAST. THE KIND OF SECOND SET THAT MAKES PEOPLE DRIVE TO LONG ISLAND. PARTS OF THIS SHOW APPEAR ON GO TO NASSAU, THE 2012 RHINO RELEASE COVERING BOTH NIGHTS.

MAY 16, 1980 — NASSAU COLISEUM. NIGHT TWO. CHINA CAT INTO I KNOW YOU RIDER CLOSES THE FIRST SET — UNUSUAL PLACEMENT FOR THE SIGNATURE PAIRING, WHICH TYPICALLY ANCHORS SET TWO. WHEN THE DEAD SHUFFLED THEIR SEQUENCING IT USUALLY MEANT THEY WERE FEELING LOOSE AND CONFIDENT. HIGH TIME APPEARS IN THE FIRST SET, A DEEP RARITY THAT GARCIA RESERVES FOR NIGHTS WHEN HE'S FEELING INTROSPECTIVE — A WORKINGMAN'S DEAD BALLAD MOST FANS HAVE NEVER HEARD LIVE. MORNING DEW EMERGES FROM THE SPACE-AND-TRUCKIN' SEQUENCE WITH FULL VOLCANIC FORCE — GARCIA'S MOST SUSTAINED EMOTIONAL CRESCENDO GIVEN MAXIMUM DRAMATIC WEIGHT BY ITS PLACEMENT. THE PLAYING IN THE BAND SANDWICH IN SET TWO WRAPS AROUND COMES A TIME — DEVASTATING TENDERNESS NESTLED INSIDE THE BAND'S MOST EXPLORATORY JAM VEHICLE. BROKEDOWN PALACE FOR THE ENCORE. PARTS OF THIS SHOW APPEAR ON GO TO NASSAU.

JUNE 20, 1980 — WEST HIGH AUDITORIUM, ANCHORAGE, ALASKA. A HIGH SCHOOL GYM. TWO THOUSAND SEATS. NINETEEN HOURS OF DAYLIGHT. THE CREW DROVE THE EQUIPMENT UP THE ALASKA HIGHWAY THROUGH CANADA TO GET HERE. NIGHT TWO OF THREE — THE SOLSTICE SHOW IS TOMORROW. A WOMAN GOT MARRIED AT A GLACIER EARLIER IN THE DAY, CHANGED CLOTHES, AND CAME TO THE SHOW. WEIR AND LESH DID A HELICOPTER TRAFFIC REPORT FOR A LOCAL RADIO STATION. THE ESTIMATED PROPHET INTO THE OTHER ONE IS THE CENTERPIECE — THE DEAD AT THEIR MOST PSYCHEDELIC IN A ROOM SMALL ENOUGH TO FEEL EVERY NOTE IN YOUR CHEST. THE MATRIX RECORDING — BLENDED SOUNDBOARD AND AUDIENCE SOURCES — IS CONSIDERED SUPERIOR TO THE STRAIGHT BOARD TAPE. SHIP OF FOOLS IN THE SECOND SET IS NINE MINUTES OF GARCIA AT HIS MOST REFLECTIVE. THE SHOW ENDS IN FULL DAYLIGHT. PETER SIMON PHOTOGRAPHED THE PERFORMANCE.

AUGUST 19, 1980 — UPTOWN THEATRE, CHICAGO. NIGHT ONE OF THREE. THE OPENER IS MISSISSIPPI HALF-STEP UPTOWN TOODELOO — WHETHER THE VENUE-NAME PUN IS GARCIA'S DRY HUMOR OR PURE COINCIDENCE, IT'S PERFECT. THE UPTOWN IS A FORTY-FOUR-HUNDRED-SEAT RAPP AND RAPP MOVIE PALACE FROM 1925, SPANISH REVIVAL ORNAMENT FLOOR TO CEILING, ONE OF THE LARGEST AND MOST OPULENT THEATERS EVER BUILT. IT WILL CLOSE NEXT YEAR AND SPEND THE NEXT FOUR DECADES IN RESTORATION LIMBO. KEITH GODCHAUX DIED TWENTY-SEVEN DAYS AGO IN A CAR ACCIDENT ON HIS THIRTY-SECOND BIRTHDAY. THE BAND IS ON TOUR AND PLAYING THROUGH IT. LITTLE RED ROOSTER OPENS THE SECOND SET — A SLOW BLUES, A RARE CHOICE, THE BAND IN A REFLECTIVE MOOD. THE PLAYING IN THE BAND SANDWICH WRAPS AROUND COMES A TIME, GARCIA'S MOST TENDER BALLAD CRADLED INSIDE THE BAND'S MOST EXPLORATORY JAM VEHICLE.

SEPTEMBER 2, 1980 — COMMUNITY WAR MEMORIAL, ROCHESTER. EYEWITNESS: "GENERAL ADMISSION, SECOND ROW, HOT AND CROWDED, PEOPLE PUSHING FROM ALL SIDES. THE FIRST NOTE OF DEW TOOK MY BREATH AWAY." ANOTHER: "FIRST ROW IN FRONT OF JERRY. ALMOST BUMPED INTO HIM AT HIS LIMO ON THE WAY OUT." A THIRD: "MY FIRST SHOW AT SEVENTEEN — RADIO SHACK CHROME TAPES AT OSWEGO STATE." THE IKO IKO OUT OF SPACE WAS "A SHOCK" BUT THE MORNING DEW THAT FOLLOWED — "WAS IT REALLY TWENTY-FIVE YEARS AGO?"

OCTOBER 4, 1980 — WARFIELD THEATRE. TEN NIGHTS INTO THE ACOUSTIC-ELECTRIC RESIDENCY. THE BAND WAS TIGHTENING — EACH NIGHT MORE CONFIDENT, THE TRANSITIONS MORE SEAMLESS. GARCIA WOULD EVENTUALLY PLAY THIS ROOM EIGHTY-EIGHT TIMES WITH VARIOUS SIDE BANDS — MORE THAN ANY OTHER VENUE.

OCTOBER 14, 1980 — WARFIELD THEATRE. THE FINAL NIGHT OF FIFTEEN. BILL GRAHAM SERVED CHAMPAGNE TO THE ENTIRE AUDIENCE. "NO DANCING" WAS THE POLICY — BUT GRAHAM PUT SPEAKERS IN THE HALLWAYS SO PEOPLE COULD DANCE AND STILL HEAR. AN EYEWITNESS: "TWO FEET FROM JERRY. THERE WAS NO RAIL." SOMEONE PLASTERED COSMIC WIMPOUT STICKERS ON THE LINEN-COVERED WALLS. A COUPLE WAS HAVING SEX ON THE MAIN STAIRCASE — GRAHAM THREW THEM OUT. AN AIDE: "C'MON BILL — SHE'S FUCKED UP AND HE'S JUST FUCKED!" GRAHAM: "WHAT AM I SUPPOSED TO DO — GIVE THEM A MEDAL?" THE MARQUEE OUTSIDE READ: "IF I KNEW THE WAY / I WOULD TAKE YOU HOME." EYEWITNESS: "FIFTEEN YEARS OLD, FRONT ROW, FIRST ACID SHOW. I STILL DREAM ABOUT IT." THE MATERIAL FROM THESE FIFTEEN NIGHTS BECAME THE ALBUMS RECKONING AND DEAD SET.

OCTOBER 26, 1980 — RADIO CITY MUSIC HALL. FIFTH OF EIGHT NIGHTS. THE LITTLE RED ROOSTER "HOWLS — ONE OF THE BEST VERSIONS I HAVE EVER HEARD." BOBBY'S HOWARD DEAN SCREAM AT THE END OF GOOD LOVIN'. EYEWITNESS: "THE WHOLE CROWD SWAYING IN UNISON DURING HE'S GONE."

OCTOBER 30, 1980 — RADIO CITY MUSIC HALL. THE NIGHT BEFORE HALLOWEEN. "THE STUFF OF LEGENDS." THE ACOUSTIC SET: "FREE AND FLOWING." THE DEEP ELEM BLUES "SWINGS." THE ON THE ROAD AGAIN IS "QUINTESSENTIAL — MORE PLEASING THAN PIGPEN'S EARLY ERA RENDERING." THE TO LAY ME DOWN IS "SUBLIME, IN ITS FINEST FORM." TOMORROW NIGHT THE CAMERAS WOULD ROLL FOR DEAD AHEAD.

OCTOBER 31, 1980 — RADIO CITY MUSIC HALL, NEW YORK. THE WORLD'S LARGEST INDOOR THEATRE — FIFTY-NINE HUNDRED AND SIXTY SEATS OF ART DECO MAGNIFICENCE, BUILT AS PART OF ROCKEFELLER CENTER IN 1932. HOME OF THE ROCKETTES. MARBLE AND GOLD FOIL MIXED WITH BAKELITE AND ALUMINUM. DONALD DESKEY'S INTERIORS. HALLOWEEN NIGHT. AL FRANKEN — THEN AN SNL ALUMNUS, DECADES BEFORE THE U.S. SENATE — INTRODUCED THE BAND. THE SHOW WAS BROADCAST ON RADIO AND SIMULCAST VIA CLOSED-CIRCUIT FEED TO FOURTEEN MOVIE THEATERS AROUND THE COUNTRY. NEW YORK CITY TOURISTS SAW DEADHEADS WAITING IN LINE ALL NIGHT AND THOUGHT THEY WERE "HOMELESS, ON A TV SHOW, OR SOME SORT OF CULT." THREE SETS: ACOUSTIC FIRST (THE FIRST ACOUSTIC DEAD SINCE THE EARLY SEVENTIES), THEN TWO ELECTRIC. RARE "LITTLE SADIE" — HADN'T BEEN PLAYED SINCE 1970. THE FINAL PERFORMANCE OF "SAGE AND SPIRIT." THE SPACE > FIRE ON THE MOUNTAIN (WITHOUT SCARLET BEGONIAS) WAS WHAT THE DEAD'S ARCHIVIST CALLED "ONE OF THE COOLER TRANSITIONS YOU'LL HEAR" — "EXTRA SPOOKY" QUALITIES. THIS BECAME THE DEAD AHEAD CONCERT FILM.

NOVEMBER 28, 1980 — LAKELAND CIVIC CENTER. THE DAY AFTER THANKSGIVING. EYEWITNESS — TEN FEET FROM GARCIA: "HE KEPT DROOLING COPIOUSLY ALL DURING THE SHOW. BOBBY HAD THICK BIRKENSTOCK SUEDE SHOES ON." THE STAGE WAS VERY LOW. "ACID WAS IN INCREDIBLY SHORT SUPPLY THAT NIGHT — PEOPLE RUNNING AROUND SHOUTING FOR IT." LITTLE KIDS APPEARED AROUND THEIR KNEES — "WE HOISTED THEM ON OUR SHOULDERS AND THEY GOT A RINGSIDE VIEW." FIRST ELECTRIC DEEP ELEM BLUES SINCE 1970. THE LET IT GROW WAS "THE JAMMY HIGHLIGHT." THE CITRUS-AND-PHOSPHATE TOWN WITH THIRTEEN FRANK LLOYD WRIGHT BUILDINGS DOWN THE ROAD. INCLUDED IN THE 30 TRIPS BOX SET.

NOVEMBER 30, 1980 — FOX THEATRE, ATLANTA. UNDER THE TWINKLING ARABIAN NIGHT SKY. AN EYEWITNESS WHO SAW THREE HUNDRED AND FORTY-THREE DEAD SHOWS: "THIS WAS THE BEST. YOU MISSED IT. DEAL WITH IT." ANOTHER WAS ALMOST THROWN OUT OF THE BALCONY FOR NOT STAYING SEATED. ANOTHER RECEIVED A MIRACLE TICKET FROM A STRANGER AFTER HIS GRANDFATHER DIED. ANOTHER STILL KNOWS EVERY NOTE FIFTEEN YEARS AFTER LOSING THE TAPE. "DURING SPACE, SOME GUY ASKS 'WHAT SONG IS THIS?'" THE SCARLET > FIRE TRANSITION: "THERE ARE THREE FALSE CLIMAXES BEFORE THE FINAL DELIRIOUS, PEAKING, RIPPING JERRY JAM INTO FIRE." ONE REVIEWER: "THE BEST SCARLET > FIRE I'VE HEARD — AND I DON'T SAY THAT LIGHTLY." RELEASED AS DAVE'S PICKS VOLUME 8.

DECEMBER 26, 1980 — OAKLAND AUDITORIUM ARENA. JOHN LENNON HAD BEEN SHOT SIXTEEN DAYS EARLIER. THE DIRE WOLF OPENER: "PLEASE DON'T MURDER ME" — THE LYRICS HAD NEVER CARRIED THIS WEIGHT BEFORE. "THE WOUNDS WERE STILL VERY FRESH."

DECEMBER 28, 1980 — OAKLAND AUDITORIUM ARENA. NIGHT THREE OF THE NEW YEAR'S RUN. TENT CITY OUTSIDE THE VENUE. "PHIL DROPPING BOMBS THROUGHOUT." THE FRANKLIN'S TOWER AND BIRD SONG: "BOTH SMOKIN'." THE ACOUSTIC SET HAD BECOME THE SOUL OF THE SHOW — WHEN THE BAND PLAYED RIPPLE AND THE WHOLE ROOM SANG ALONG, JERRY'S VOICE CARRIED ABOVE IT ALL.

DECEMBER 31, 1980 — OAKLAND AUDITORIUM ARENA. NEW YEAR'S EVE. THREE SETS — ACOUSTIC FIRST, THEN TWO ELECTRIC. THE CONTINUATION OF THE FORMAT BORN AT THE WARFIELD AND RADIO CITY. TENT CITY OUTSIDE THE AUDITORIUM. ONE FIRST-TIMER WAS "STRUCK BY THE EXISTENCE OF TEENAGE HIPPIES AT THAT LATE DATE." DURING THE ACOUSTIC SET, WITH THE WHOLE AUDITORIUM "SURGING, CLAPPING AND SINGING ALONG WITH RIPPLE, JERRY'S VOICE CARRIED ABOVE IT ALL WITH 'IF I KNEW THE WAY, I WOULD TAKE YOU HOME...'" BLAIR JACKSON, THE DEAD'S HISTORIAN, NOTED HE MISSED THIS RUN WHILE IN MANHATTAN AND CALCULATED THAT "THE DEAD MIGHT HAVE BEEN PLAYING CASSIDY DURING THEIR ACOUSTIC SET AT THE MOMENT OF MIDNIGHT ON THE EAST COAST." BILL GRAHAM RODE IN ON A GIANT SKULL AT MIDNIGHT. THE ENCORE: SATISFACTION AND BROKEDOWN PALACE. A ROCK AND ROLL SONG FROM THE ROLLING STONES AND A COUNTRY HYMN FROM ROBERT HUNTER. THAT WAS THE DEAD.
